Database Connections¶

Database connection module for PostgreSQL, MongoDB, and MinIO.

Provides connection classes for all three storage systems used in the coursework data pipeline.

class modules.db.db_connection.PostgresConnection(host, port, database, user, password)[source]¶

Bases: object

Manages connections and queries to the PostgreSQL database.

Parameters:

  • host – Database host address.

  • port – Database port number.

  • database – Name of the database.

  • user – Database username.

  • password – Database password.

read_query(query, params=None)[source]¶

Execute a SELECT query and return results as a DataFrame.

Parameters:

  • query – SQL query string.

  • params – Optional dictionary of query parameters.

Returns:

Query results.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

execute(query, params=None)[source]¶

Execute a non-returning SQL statement (CREATE, INSERT, etc.).

Parameters:

  • query – SQL statement string.

  • params – Optional dictionary of query parameters.

write_dataframe(df, table_name, schema, if_exists='append')[source]¶

Write a DataFrame to a PostgreSQL table.

Parameters:

  • df – DataFrame to write.

  • table_name – Target table name.

  • schema – Target schema name.

  • if_exists – What to do if table exists (‘append’, ‘replace’, ‘fail’).

write_dataframe_on_conflict_do_nothing(df, table_name, schema, conflict_columns)[source]¶

Write rows to PostgreSQL using ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING.

Parameters:

  • df – DataFrame to write.

  • table_name – Target table name.

  • schema – Target schema name.

  • conflict_columns – Columns defining the conflict target.

get_company_list()[source]¶

Retrieve the full company universe from company_static.

Returns:

All companies with symbol, security, sector, etc.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

get_managed_symbol_tables(schema='team_wittgenstein')[source]¶

Return tables in the managed schema that include a symbol column.

get_tracked_symbols(schema='team_wittgenstein')[source]¶

Return distinct symbols currently present in managed tables.

delete_symbol_data(symbols, schema='team_wittgenstein')[source]¶

Delete symbols from all managed schema tables that carry a symbol.

delete_symbols_missing_from_company_list(current_symbols)[source]¶

Delete managed data for symbols no longer present in company_static.

test_connection()[source]¶

Test the database connection.

Returns:

True if connection is successful.

Return type:

bool

execute_sql_file(sql_path: str)[source]¶
class modules.db.db_connection.MongoConnection(host, port)[source]¶

Bases: object

Manages connections and operations on the MongoDB database.

Parameters:

  • host – MongoDB host address.

  • port – MongoDB port number.

insert_one(db_name, collection, document)[source]¶

Insert a single document into a collection.

Parameters:

  • db_name – Database name.

  • collection – Collection name.

  • document – Dictionary to insert.

Returns:

The inserted document’s ID.

insert_many(db_name, collection, documents)[source]¶

Insert multiple documents into a collection.

Parameters:

  • db_name – Database name.

  • collection – Collection name.

  • documents – List of dictionaries to insert.

Returns:

List of inserted document IDs.

find(db_name, collection, query=None)[source]¶

Query documents from a collection.

Parameters:

  • db_name – Database name.

  • collection – Collection name.

  • query – Optional filter dictionary. Returns all if None.

Returns:

Matching documents.

Return type:

list

test_connection()[source]¶

Test the MongoDB connection.

Returns:

True if connection is successful.

Return type:

bool

class modules.db.db_connection.MinioConnection(host, access_key, secret_key, secure=False)[source]¶

Bases: object

Manages connections and file operations on MinIO object storage.

Parameters:

  • host – MinIO host address (host:port).

  • access_key – MinIO access key.

  • secret_key – MinIO secret key.

  • secure – Whether to use HTTPS. Defaults to False for local dev.

upload_json(bucket, object_name, data)[source]¶

Upload a Python object as JSON to MinIO.

Parameters:

  • bucket – Target bucket name.

  • object_name – Object key/path in the bucket.

  • data – Python object serialisable to JSON.

download_json(bucket, object_name)[source]¶

Download a JSON object from MinIO and parse it.

Parameters:

  • bucket – Source bucket name.

  • object_name – Object key/path in the bucket.

Returns:

Parsed Python object, or None if not found.

upload_dataframe(bucket, object_name, df)[source]¶

Upload a DataFrame as Parquet to MinIO.

Parameters:

  • bucket – Target bucket name.

  • object_name – Object key/path in the bucket.

  • df – DataFrame to upload.

download_dataframe(bucket, object_name)[source]¶

Download a Parquet file from MinIO as a DataFrame.

Parameters:

  • bucket – Source bucket name.

  • object_name – Object key/path in the bucket.

Returns:

pd.DataFrame, or None if not found.

list_objects(bucket, prefix=None)[source]¶

List objects in a bucket with an optional prefix filter.

Parameters:

  • bucket – Bucket name.

  • prefix – Optional prefix to filter objects.

Returns:

Object names matching the prefix.

Return type:

list

object_exists(bucket, object_name)[source]¶

Check if an object exists in a bucket.

Parameters:

  • bucket – Bucket name.

  • object_name – Object key/path.

Returns:

True if the object exists.

Return type:

bool

delete_object(bucket, object_name)[source]¶

Delete an object if it exists in the bucket.

test_connection()[source]¶

Test the MinIO connection.

Returns:

True if connection is successful.

Return type:

bool
